title: Type Narrowing & Guards
description: Deeply understanding exactly what a variable is at runtime.

slides.title: The Union Dilemma 🔀
slides.content:
Imagine you have a function that accepts an argument that might be a `string | number`.

Inside the function, you want to call `.toUpperCase()`. But TypeScript yells at you! Why? Because a `number` does not have a `.toUpperCase()` method.

slides.title: Narrowing with `typeof`
slides.content: The simplest way to fix this is by 'Narrowing' the type down. We do this by writing standard JavaScript conditional logic that structurally guarantees what type it currently is.

quizData.question: Does TypeScript structurally drop and ignore your type annotations inside an `if (typeof...)` check?

options.text: Yes, it safely assumes it's 'any' cleanly inside the conditionally scoped block.
No! It dynamically analyzes your exact control flow and 'narrows' the union type automatically.

quizData.explanation: TypeScript is deeply intelligent; it follows your standard JavaScript `if/else` control flow to perfectly narrow types down in real-time.

slides.title: Type Guards (Custom)
slides.content: For complex objects, `typeof` isn't enough (everything is just an `object`). You can write special 'Type Guard' functions that return a boolean, using the magical `value is Type` syntax.

slides.title: Quest Complete! 🛡️
slides.content: You now understand Type Narrowing and Guards! You can confidently accept massive Union Types and safely slice them down to handle explicitly exactly what you uniquely expertly cleanly need!
